item.title: Are Graham Platner's scandals helping him?
item.description: Are Graham Platner's controversies actually helping him in the Maine Senate race? In this episode of "Make It Make Sense," Kate Andrews talks with Carine Hajjar about Platner's rise, his refusal to drop out amid multiple scandals, and why Democrats are still standing behind him.

item.title: Build homes, don’t seize them, Mayor Mamdani
item.description: The best way to alleviate the “deepening crisis” is to stop digging a hole with government control.

item.title: Europe is becoming Iran’s playground for terrorism
item.description: Tehran is taking a page out of Moscow’s playbook for hybrid aggression in the West.

item.title: How to stop the next pandemic before it begins
item.description: Deadly pathogens can come from nature — or from the lab. That’s why risky research needs guardrails.

item.title: Big Tobacco didn’t just sell cigarettes. It shaped what Americans eat.
item.description: New research shows how cigarette companies brought their addiction playbook to the food industry.

item.title: A ‘forgotten world war’ helped forge America
item.description: The Revolutionary War succeeded because of allies at home and abroad.

item.title: Elon Musk took out his chainsaw. Government took out its checkbook.
item.description: The SpaceX IPO lays bare Musk’s greatest contradiction.

item.title: Rising measles cases offer a dire warning
item.description: Once-eliminated infectious diseases are spinning out of control following attacks on vaccines.

item.title: Graham Platner has a Kik account. Here’s what you should know about that app.
item.description: Predators on Kik pose a direct threat to the children of the very Maine voters Platner seeks to represent.

item.title: D.C. has a ‘teen takeover’ problem
item.description: ‘Teen takeovers’ are becoming a defining issue in the D.C. mayoral race. In this episode of “Make It Make Sense,” James Hohmann and Rob Gebelhoff break down where the candidates stand on crime and policing, how they may approach the National Guard in D.C., and whether they’ll work with or against President Donald Trump.

item.title: America needs more data centers. New York wants fewer.
item.description: Kathy Hochul has a chance to block the anti-growth data center moratorium.

item.title: The U.S. now faces Europe’s defense dilemma
item.description: Israeli weapons are field-tested and effective. Will the U.S. forgo them for moral posturing?

item.title: Anthropic might be the most powerful company in the world
item.description: Leaders in business, government and religion are all paying attention.

item.title: What Ben Franklin’s ‘Drinker’s Dictionary’ tells Americans today
item.description: The man who handed down “swallow’d a Tavern Token” knew we'd never be perfect.

item.title: My purple district can tell you what the Democratic autopsy left out
item.description: People in districts like mine need a helping hand, not a lecture.
